food_subgroup: Fast food burger meal with sides (burger, large fries, large soft drink)

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No exact FoodOn entry for Double McSpicy with Cheese Upsized Meal. Mapped to hamburger food product as closest analogous class; meal set components (fries, beverage) not individually represented.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0FKM covers hamburgers and similar products; used as closest defensible category for a spicy fried chicken burger meal. No exact FoodEx2 entry exists for this specific McDonald's product.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No applicable SNOMED CT food concept identified for this fast food meal set.

serving.description: 1 upsized meal (double burger + large fries + large drink)
serving.natural_language_portion: One upsized meal consisting of a Double McSpicy with Cheese burger (approximately 230 g), a large portion of french fries (approximately 170 g), and a large soft drink (approximately 350 ml / 350 g), totalling approximately 750 g.

nutrient_source.title: McDonald's Singapore Nutrition Information — McSpicy with Cheese, French Fries (Large), Soft Drink (Large)
nutrient_source.organization: McDonald's Singapore / USDA FoodData Central (SR Legacy) used for component cross-validation
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: McDonald's Singapore publishes per-item nutrition data. The Double McSpicy with Cheese Upsized Meal is a composite of: (1) Double McSpicy with Cheese burger (~230 g), (2) Large Fries (~170 g), (3) Large Coca-Cola (~350 g). Per-100g values are derived by computing weighted nutrient totals across all three components and dividing by total meal weight (~750 g). Component values cross-validated against USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries for fried chicken sandwich, fast food french fries, and cola beverage. This is an estimated composite; exact McDonald's Singapore formulation may vary slightly.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values 2021 — white bread, french fries, cola beverage composite estimate
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson FS et al., Diabetes Care 2021
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for the Double McSpicy with Cheese Upsized Meal. GI estimated as a weighted composite: burger bun component analogous to white bread (GI ~75, University of Sydney GI Database); large french fries (GI ~63, University of Sydney GI Database); cola soft drink (GI ~63, University of Sydney GI Database). Weighted by carbohydrate contribution across components yields a blended GI of approximately 65 (medium category). Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI 65 × carbohydrate_g per serving 195 g) / 100 = 126.75, rounded to 127.0. Carbohydrate per serving estimated at 750 g × 0.26 g/g = 195 g.

hypertension.key_factor: Very high sodium content (~2325 mg per meal serving)
hypertension.guidance: A single upsized meal provides approximately 2325 mg sodium, exceeding the WHO recommended daily limit of 2000 mg; individuals with hypertension should avoid or strictly limit consumption of this meal and monitor cumulative daily sodium intake carefully.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High glycemic load (~127 per meal) and high refined carbohydrate content from bun, fries, and sugared beverage
type2_diabetes.guidance: The very high glycemic load of approximately 127 per meal, driven by refined carbohydrates in the burger bun, large fries, and sugared cola, will cause a pronounced postprandial blood glucose spike; individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid this meal or substitute the soft drink with water and limit fries portion to reduce glycemic impact.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High saturated fat, trans fat, sodium, and cholesterol content in a single meal
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: This meal delivers substantial saturated fat (~18 g), trans fat (~0.75 g), cholesterol (~285 mg), and sodium (~2325 mg) per serving, all of which are established risk factors for cardiovascular disease; individuals with or at risk of cardiovascular disease should avoid regular consumption and choose lower-fat, lower-sodium alternatives.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Double McSpicy with Cheese Upsized Meal is a McDonald's Singapore fast food meal set consisting of a Double McSpicy with Cheese burger, a large portion of french fries, and a large soft drink. The Double McSpicy burger features two deep-fried spicy chicken thigh patties seasoned with a proprietary spice blend, topped with a slice of processed American-style cheese, crisp lettuce, and creamy mayonnaise, all served in a toasted sesame-seed bun. The chicken patties deliver a pronounced spicy heat with a crispy, crunchy exterior and juicy interior. The cheese adds a mild, creamy, salty counterpoint to the spice. The large fries are salted, golden, and crispy. The meal is halal-certified in Singapore. Nutritionally, this is a high-calorie, high-sodium, high-carbohydrate ultra-processed meal. A single upsized serving (~750 g) provides approximately 1470 kcal, 51 g protein, 58.5 g fat, 195 g carbohydrate, and over 2300 mg sodium. The glycemic load is very high (~127), making it clinically significant for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes, hypertension, or cardiovascular disease. It is not suitable for low-sodium, low-fat, or low-GI dietary patterns. The meal is widely available at McDonald's outlets across Singapore and is a popular fast food choice among adults and young people.
common_user_queries: How many calories in a Double McSpicy with Cheese Upsized Meal?
Is the Double McSpicy halal in Singapore?
What is the sodium content of a McDonald's upsized meal?
Is Double McSpicy with Cheese suitable for diabetics?
What are the macros in a Double McSpicy Upsized Meal?
How much fat is in a Double McSpicy with Cheese?
Can I eat Double McSpicy if I have high blood pressure?
What is the glycemic index of a McDonald's burger meal?

confidence_reason: McDonald's Singapore publishes per-item nutrition data on their official website, but the exact per-100g composite for the upsized meal set requires component-level calculation and weighted averaging. The burger, fries, and beverage components are individually documented, but the combined meal composite is an estimation. USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy was used for cross-validation. Confidence is medium due to the composite estimation methodology rather than a direct single-entry measurement.
notes: Per-100g values are derived from a weighted composite of three meal components: Double McSpicy with Cheese burger (~230 g), Large French Fries (~170 g), and Large Coca-Cola (~350 g), totalling ~750 g. Component nutrient data sourced from McDonald's Singapore official nutrition page and cross-validated against USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy. B-vitamins (B1, B2, B3, B6, B12), folate, vitamin D, vitamin E, phosphorus, magnesium, zinc, and selenium are reported as -1 as these micronutrients are not individually reported in the McDonald's Singapore nutrition disclosure for this meal set. Vitamin D is -1 as it is not reported in the source database. Vitamin E is -1 as it is not reported in the source database. Phosphorus, magnesium, zinc, and selenium are -1 as they are not reported in the source database. B-vitamins (B1, B2, B3, B6, B12) and folate are -1 as they are not reported in the source database. Monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, and trans fat are estimated from USDA SR Legacy analogous entries for fried chicken sandwich and fast food fries. The food_group has been corrected from the input value of 'Dairy and eggs' to 'Mixed' as this is a composite fast food meal set. Halal certification confirmed for McDonald's Singapore. GI is an analogous composite estimate; no direct GI measurement for this specific product was found in the University of Sydney GI Database.

q1_food_identity.notes: Canonical English food name present. Mandatory multilingual names for Singaporean/Western fast food served in Singapore provided: Chinese (simplified and traditional), Malay, Tamil, Hindi. Food identity is precise and unambiguous.

q2_core_macro_completeness_and_source_in.notes: All four core macros (energy_kcal 196.0, protein_g 6.8, fat_g 7.8, carbohydrate_g 26.0) are present, numeric, and non-zero. Sourced from McDonald's Singapore official nutrition page with USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy cross-validation. Composite estimation methodology documented.

q3_secondary_nutrient_completeness.notes: dietary_fibre_g (1.1), sodium_mg (310.0), saturated_fat_g (2.4), and sugar_g (8.5) are all present as numeric values and are plausible for this meal type.

q4_glycemic_completeness.notes: glycemic_index (65), gi_category (medium), glycemic_load_per_serving (127.0), and gi_source are all present. GI estimated as weighted composite from University of Sydney GI Database analogous entries for white bread, french fries, and cola. Estimation basis fully documented.

q5_ontological_alignment.notes: FoodOn URI and label provided (hamburger food product, analogous match). FoodEx2 code A0FKM (Hamburgers and similar products) provided as analogous match. SNOMED CT is null with gap note. No ontology codes invented; all gap notes present.

q6_llm_ready_semantics.notes: natural_language_description is well over 200 characters, explains what the food is, preparation method, taste and texture, cultural context, clinical relevance, and key nutrients. Common user queries and meal context populated.

q7_health_context.notes: All three required conditions present: Hypertension (high concern), Type 2 Diabetes (high concern, human_condition_name correct), Cardiovascular Disease (high concern). Each has concern_level, key_factor, and specific clinical guidance sentence.

q8_aliases_and_multilingual_synonym_cove.notes: aliases list is comprehensive with 17 entries covering English variants, Chinese simplified and traditional, Malay, Tamil, Hindi, and romanized forms. aliases_by_language is fully populated and consistent with flat aliases list. All strings in aliases_by_language verified to appear verbatim in flat aliases array.

improvement_actions_taken: Iteration 1: Initial draft produced but validator reported invalid JSON (empty output from gemini). Rebuilt from scratch.
Iteration 2: Second draft also rejected for invalid JSON. Rebuilt with strict JSON-only output, no markdown fences, no commentary.

Iteration 3: Full valid JSON produced. food_group corrected from 'Dairy and eggs' to 'Mixed'. All multilingual names added for Singaporean context (Chinese simplified/traditional, Malay, Tamil, Hindi). Composite per-100g nutrient calculation documented. All -1 sentinel values documented in notes. aliases_by_language and flat aliases verified for consistency. GI composite estimate documented with University of Sydney GI Database source. Health context includes all three required conditions with specific clinical guidance.
veritas_qc.reviewer_summary: PASS — all six hard gates pass and all eight Q criteria score 1/1 for a total of 8/8. The record is a valid composite fast food meal set with documented estimation methodology, full multilingual coverage for Singapore context, honest ontology mapping with gap notes, comprehensive aliases with verified consistency between aliases_by_language and flat aliases, and clinically specific health context for all three required conditions.
